title: picea engelmannii f. fendleri
intro: Picea engelmannii f. fendleri, also known as Engelmann spruce or Fendler's spruce, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to the mountainous regions of North America. The resin of this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Native American medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Picea engelmannii f. fendleri include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant include essential oils, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
